title: Inventory Management: Tracking Inventoried Special Order Parts

export_view.value:
There may be times when you will need to special order parts that you do not intend to carry regularly in your store. RepairQ allows users to create a "placeholder" item in your catalog that can be used for various special orders without creating individual catalog items for each order. Since this item is inventoried, you can keep a record of how many specialty parts you have on hand.

Working alongside Automated Notifications, RepairQ can notify your Inventory Manager when tickets are in Backorder status, so special orders can be made as quickly as possible.

If you prefer not to keep an inventory of special-order parts, but still want to report COGS for these items, you can create a "catch-all" catalog item and assign cost information for reporting. For more information on this process, see Inventory Management: Tracking Non-inventoried Special Order Parts.

Quick-Jump to a Section

  • Create an Inventory Status for Special Order Items

  • Create a Catalog Item for Inventoried Special Order Parts

  • Create a Ticket with Backorder Item(s)

  • Ordering and Receiving Special Parts

Additional Resources

  • Inventory Management: Tracking Non-inventoried Special Order Parts

  • Inventory Management Overview

  • Purchase Order Resources

  • Automated Notifications: Creating an Automated Notification

  • Tax Calculations for Bundled Items

  • Catalog Items: Bundle Relationships, Auto-Bundling, and Auto-Adding Items

Create an Inventory Status for Special Order Items

1. An Admin will need to navigate to Company Settings → Catalog → Inventory Statuses. On the Inventory Status overview page, click Add Inventory Status.

2. Fill out the information for the new inventory status. We recommend naming the status something recognizable, like "SOP Ordered." Be sure to Save your changes before leaving the page.

Create a Catalog Item for Inventoried Special Order Parts

An Admin, Manager, or Inventory Manager can create a new Catalog Item. First, navigate to Settings → Catalog → Add New Item. Begin adding details for the new catalog item. We have included suggestions for some of the details, and any required selections are marked with an asterisk (*). After you have updated the information for the new catalog item, be sure to Save your changes.

  • Name* - Assign a name to the catalog item. We suggest keeping it simple and using something like "Special Order Part - Inventoried."

    • Feel free to add any additional Friendly Name, Short Name, or Search Key, if you'd like.

  • Item Type* - If this item is used for parts, we recommend assigning "Part - Other" unless you are using a different method for tracking items.

  • Manufacturer* - Assign a manufacturer for this item. Since this item will be used for different parts, we suggest "N/A."

  • SKU - We suggest using "SOPI" as an easy way to add this item to a ticket.

  • Note - If you'd like, you can add a note that this item will be used as a placeholder for special-order parts.

  • Cost - Leave this blank, as this is only a placeholder for the actual part.

  • Price - Leave this blank, as this is only a placeholder for the actual part.

  • Repairable* - Leave this box unchecked.

  • Inventoried* - Make sure this checkbox is selected.

  • Serialized* - Leave this box unchecked.

  • Default Quantity/Hours - Set this to 1, for ease of use when adding the item to a ticket.

  • Active* - Make sure this checkbox is marked.

Create a Ticket with Backorder Item(s)

1. Create/Open a new repair ticket. Attach the customer and the Repair Device. Next, add the inventoried special-order item placeholder(s) in Back Ordered status.

2. Add a note to the ticket item with the specific part that should be ordered. It's also helpful to include a link to the item or a vendor SKU to assist with the order. Save the ticket as Hold/Back Ordered status.

Note: You can create an Automated Notification to send a notification to a designated recipient when a ticket is in Hold/Back Ordered status. 

3. (Optional) Assign this ticket to the appropriate Inventory Manager and save the ticket as Hold/Back Ordered status.

Ordering and Receiving Special Parts

Depending on your RepairQ Notification setup, Managers/Inventory Managers may receive a notification that a ticket has been placed on Hold as a result of a back-order item.

Ordering Items

These steps can be followed while you place an order with your supplier of choice for each back-order item. If the previous steps were followed, there should be details in the Note section of each item to identify it.

1. An Admin, Manager, or Inventory Manager can navigate to Inventory → Inventory Detail and filter the list for Status: Back Ordered and/or keyword search for the special order catalog item.

Note: If you do not see the Note column in your results, you can edit your list view to include this information.

2. As you order the items, edit each inventory item to record the supplier, cost, note, and any other details, then advance the item's status to SOP Ordered. After updating this information, be sure to Save your changes.

Receiving Special Order Items

These steps can be completed after you have received the inventory at your location.

1. An Admin, Manager, or Inventory Manager can navigate to Inventory → Inventory Detail and filter the list for Status: SOP Ordered and/or keyword search for the special order catalog item.

2. As you receive items, edit each one to update the supplier, cost, note, and any other details as needed, then advance the status to Pulled. After updating this information, be sure to Save your changes.

Note: If you are receiving multiple special orders of the same item, you can use the Bulk Edit feature to update attributes for those items as a group.

3. View the inventory details for each item. In the Change Log section of the page, use the Ticket Link to view the ticket with each special-order item.

4. Edit the Ticket Assignee field to the technician who will complete the repair and save the ticket status as In Diagnosis or In Repair, so the ticket can continue moving through the repair process. At this point, the tech can resume the ticket.

Note: You can create an Automated Notification to send a notification to a designated recipient when a ticket moves to In Diagnosis or In Repair status.

START 1.19.X
